sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_date DATE, timezone_offset INTEGER, nullable_col DATE);
SELECT id, event_date, timezone_hour(event_date) AS hour_offset FROM test_dates WHERE timezone_hour(event_date) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, event_date, timezone_hour(event_date - INTERVAL (timezone_offset) HOUR) AS adjusted_hour FROM test_dates WHERE timezone_offset IS NOT NULL ORDER BY adjusted_hour;

WITH date_info AS (SELECT id, event_date, timezone_hour(event_date) AS hour FROM test_dates WHERE event_date < CURRENT_DATE) SELECT id, hour, hour - INTERVAL 1 HOUR AS adjusted_hour FROM date_info HAVING adjusted_hour IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (timezone_hour(event_date) - INTERVAL 0 HOUR) AS same_hour FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL;

SELECT t1.id, t2.event_date, t1.timezone_offset - INTERVAL 1 HOUR AS adjusted_offset FROM test_dates t1 JOIN test_dates t2 ON t1.id <> t2.id WHERE t1.timezone_offset IS NOT NULL AND t2.event_date IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE additional_dates (date1 DATE, date2 DATE);
INSERT INTO additional_dates VALUES (DATE '2020-01-01', DATE '2020-06-01'), (DATE '2019-12-31', NULL), (NULL, DATE '2022-01-01');
SELECT ad.date1, ad.date2, timezone_hour(ad.date1 - INTERVAL 5 HOUR) AS adjusted_hour1, timezone_hour(ad.date2) AS hour2 FROM additional_dates ad WHERE ad.date1 IS NOT NULL OR ad.date2 IS NOT NULL;
